page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU runs at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 4 MB L3 cache. It supports hardware-accelerated instructions such as AVX, AES, and SHA_NI.

This instance is integrated with the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a bundled 75 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume. This local storage provides low-latency, high-speed direct-attached disk access. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks indicate mixed capabilities. Single-threaded operations show moderate performance, with gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core scores landing in the average tier. However, multi-core and memory-intensive benchmarks show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU score, and Geekbench multi-core score fall into the poor category. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with large block read and write speeds categorized as weak. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the bottom tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5ad.large offers a low-cost entry point into dedicated compute-optimized hardware with local NVMe SSD storage. It avoids the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances while providing a physical core allocation. Due to its low memory capacity and modest multi-core performance, this instance is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, development environments, or small-scale batch processing workloads that benefit from fast local scratch space but do not require intensive parallel processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, 4.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a bundled 75 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities in gzip compression and Geekbench tests, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall into the poor to weak categories. This resource profile makes the c5ad.large a cost-efficient option for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic development environments, and small-scale batch processing tasks that require fast local scratch storage without demanding high memory capacity or heavy parallel compute performance.

tagline: An entry-level compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Bundled 75 GB local NVMe SSD instance store for low-latency scratch space
Dedicated CPU allocation on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Average single-core performance in compression and Geekbench tests
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and development environments
